rationale: Both correctly latch onto the thread's one hard fact (yenuo26's attribution to #4834) and give the same plausible technical story (level-2 sleep intentionally has no wake path, test should use level=1), but both then confidently declare the fix already merged and the issue closeable — unsupported by the actual thread, which ends with Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI run, i.e. still unresolved. That overreach caps correctness for both. Y is more complete, tying the diagnosis to practical runtime guidance (use level=1, note the partial-wake admission guard) and giving a fuller narrative, though it also invents more unverifiable specifics (exact merge date, 'Repro A/B/C' labels) that read as embellishment. X is tighter but thinner, and its 'already fixed' claim sits oddly next to its own 'workaround for the old commit' section.
